criteria.name: controls_are_keyboard_accessible
criteria.description: All interactive controls are keyboard accessible with Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, or Space.
criteria.reasoning: Native buttons and inputs are tabbable; keyboard focus reached the Commands button. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: modals_manage_focus
criteria.description: Modal dialogs trap focus and return it to the originating control on close.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Opening Help left focus on the originating Help button, and Tab moved focus to Sort outside the dialog. Evidence: modal-focus-shared.png

criteria.name: images_and_icons_have_alt_text
criteria.description: All images and icons have descriptive alt text.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Multiple rendered SVG icons had neither aria-hidden nor an accessible label. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: feedback_uses_live_regions
criteria.description: Errors and notifications are conveyed both visually and via ARIA live regions.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered page includes assertive status and polite live-region containers for feedback. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: forms_have_explicit_labels
criteria.description: All form elements use explicit label elements.
criteria.reasoning: All inspected inputs had associated label elements. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_follow_logical_order
criteria.description: Headings follow a logical order with no skips, such as H1 to H2 to H3.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The rendered heading sequence begins with H2 before H1 and includes repeated H3 groups. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: landmark_navigation_is_present
criteria.description: Skip-to-content or landmark navigation is present.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered UI includes header, main, nav with Workspace sections label, and aside landmarks. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: text_and_controls_have_contrast
criteria.description: All text and controls have sufficient color contrast.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered dark-theme text and controls were clearly readable with strong contrast in the desktop view. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: semantic_html_roles_are_used
criteria.description: Semantic HTML roles such as nav, main, and button are used.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered UI uses semantic header, main, nav, aside, button, input, and dialog elements. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_is_respected
criteria.description: Animations respect the prefers-reduced-motion setting.
criteria.reasoning: In the reduced-motion browser, prefers-reduced-motion matched true and computed animation/transition duration was reduced to 1e-05s. Evidence: reduced-motion-shared.png

criteria.name: controls_unreachable_by_keyboard
criteria.description: Controls cannot be activated or reached via keyboard.
criteria.reasoning: No unreachable interactive controls were observed; native controls were present in the tab sequence. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: focus_indicators_missing_or_obscured
criteria.description: Focus indicators are missing or obscured.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL condition not observed: keyboard focus produced a visible browser outline on the Commands button. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: custom_controls_lack_semantics
criteria.description: Custom components use non-semantic tags and lack ARIA roles or labels.
criteria.reasoning: No non-native clickable or tabindex-only controls were observed; interactive elements used native buttons/inputs. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: state_uses_color_only
criteria.description: Color-only indicators are used for error or state.
criteria.reasoning: State indicators include text such as allowed, forbidden, unapproved, and blocker counts in addition to color. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: modals_fail_to_trap_focus
criteria.description: Modals do not trap keyboard focus.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL condition observed: while the Help dialog was open, Tab moved focus to Sort outside the dialog. Evidence: modal-focus-shared.png

criteria.name: poor_contrast_makes_text_unreadable
criteria.description: Text is unreadable due to poor contrast.
criteria.reasoning: No unreadable contrast was observed; primary text, muted text, buttons, and status badges remained legible. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_are_visual_only
criteria.description: Error feedback is visual only and is not screen-reader accessible.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL condition not observed: the rendered app includes assertive status and polite aria-live regions for feedback. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: media_is_missing_alt_text
criteria.description: Icon or image content is missing alt text.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL condition observed: rendered SVG icon elements lacked descriptive labels or aria-hidden on multiple icons. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: heading_order_is_skipped
criteria.description: Headings are skipped or unordered, such as jumping from H1 to H3.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL condition observed: headings begin with H2 before H1, followed by H3 sections. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: animations_ignore_reduced_motion
criteria.description: Animations ignore reduced-motion settings.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL condition not observed: reduced-motion mode collapsed animation and transition durations to 1e-05s. Evidence: reduced-motion-shared.png

criteria.name: accessibility_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in accessibility that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant accessibility defect was observed beyond the covered modal focus, heading order, and icon-label issues. Evidence: desktop-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: multi_facet_round_trip
criteria.description: Multi-facet round-trip: configure several independent facets (e.g. active view, a membership change, a sort order, a created item), then reload. In persistence genres, all facets survive the reload; in in-memory genres, all facets coherently reset to the seeded baseline — never a mix.
criteria.reasoning: yes — after sorting and inserting a clip, reload reset both facets coherently to the seeded baseline (ascending sort and original clips). Evidence: multi_facet_round_trip-shared.png

criteria.name: sort_reversal_proves_live_data
criteria.description: Sort-reversal proof: sort a collection ascending, note the order, then sort descending; the order reverses correctly relative to ascending, proving sort is derived from live data rather than two hardcoded orders.
criteria.reasoning: yes — ascending order began with hashes 59d1bd…/8d0bb3…, descending changed to 944911…/3b82a…, then ascending restored the original order. Evidence: sort_reversal_proves_live_data-after.png

criteria.name: derived_view_responds_to_input
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: change an input (timeframe, filter, category) and confirm the derived view (chart, total, list) changes meaningfully rather than redrawing identically regardless of input.
criteria.reasoning: yes — changing the sort direction meaningfully changed the rendered source order. Evidence: sort_reversal_proves_live_data-after.png

criteria.name: cross_view_echo_without_reload
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: make a change in one view and confirm the same datum updates in a second, structurally different view (list ↔ detail, board ↔ stats) without reload.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The timeline clip-selection gesture was intercepted by the clip overlay, so a complete edit-and-confirm workflow across views could not be exercised. Evidence: behavioral_catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: count_delta_is_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: measure a count immediately before and immediately after the action that changes it; the delta matches exactly with no off-by-one or lagging counter.
criteria.reasoning: yes — the timeline clip count increased from 5 to 6 immediately after one Insert action. Evidence: count_delta_is_exact-shared.png

criteria.name: different_inputs_change_outcomes
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: perform the same workflow twice with different inputs and confirm the two outcomes differ in the ways the inputs dictate.
criteria.reasoning: yes — the same sort workflow with ascending versus descending input produced different source orders. Evidence: sort_reversal_proves_live_data-after.png

criteria.name: interleaved_flows_preserve_state
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: interleave two flows (e.g. start creating item A, switch views, finish creating item B, return) and confirm neither flow corrupted the other's state.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The required interleaved edit workflow could not be completed because timeline clip interaction was blocked by an overlaid span. Evidence: behavioral_catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_to_repopulated_round_trip
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: drive a collection to empty, then repopulate; derived surfaces (totals, charts, badges) track through both transitions.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Timeline deletion could not be completed because clip/remove controls were not pointer-accessible, so the empty-to-repopulated round trip could not be verified. Evidence: empty_to_repopulated_round_trip-before.png

criteria.name: partial_persistence_is_incoherent
criteria.description: A facet of user-configured state silently reverts while others survive, producing incoherent partial persistence.
criteria.reasoning: no — reload showed a coherent reset of the configured sort and inserted clip, not a partial mixed state. Evidence: multi_facet_round_trip-shared.png

criteria.name: derived_surface_ignores_inputs
criteria.description: A derived surface renders identically regardless of its inputs.
criteria.reasoning: no — the source list visibly changed when the sort input changed. Evidence: sort_reversal_proves_live_data-after.png

criteria.name: different_inputs_produce_identical_output
criteria.description: Repeating an action with different inputs yields byte-identical output.
criteria.reasoning: no — ascending and descending inputs produced different rendered hash orders. Evidence: sort_reversal_proves_live_data-after.png

criteria.name: cross_view_state_is_stale
criteria.description: State changes appear in one view but not another until reload.
criteria.reasoning: no — no completed cross-view datum comparison demonstrated stale state; the workflow was blocked before confirmation. Evidence: behavioral_catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: behavioral_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in behavioral or state-integrity flows that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: yes — timeline clip buttons and Remove controls were visibly present but pointer interaction timed out because a clip-label span intercepted pointer events, preventing normal selection/deletion workflows. Evidence: behavioral_catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_1
criteria.description: The app provides a Source Bin listing 24 deterministic source clips with duration, speaker/type, transcript snippet, rights state, and immutable media hash.
criteria.reasoning: Observed Source Bin entries Clip 1 through Clip 24 with durations, speaker/type, transcript snippets, rights states, and immutable hash labels. Evidence: cf_1-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_2
criteria.description: Users create timeline clip instances from source in/out ranges by dragging or clicking Insert, without mutating source media state.
criteria.reasoning: Clicked Insert for Clip 1; a bounded 0–10000 ms dialogue instance appeared, with history stating source untouched and hash unchanged. Evidence: cf_2-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_3
criteria.description: Selecting a timeline instance highlights exact transcript tokens, rights record, source waveform, and other instances of the same source.
criteria.reasoning: Selected Clip 1 showed transcript tokens, rights record, deterministic waveform, source hash, and two other instances. Evidence: cf_2-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_4
criteria.description: The board has dialogue, music, ambient, and marker lanes supporting drag, trim, split, ripple move, nonripple move, gap close, mute, fade, and crossfade.
criteria.reasoning: Timeline visibly provides Dialogue, Crosstalk, Music, Ambient, and Marker lanes plus trim, split, move, ripple, gap-close, mute, crossfade, and remove controls. Evidence: cf_2-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_5
criteria.description: Timeline operations use integer milliseconds and snap to transcript token boundaries, 10-ms increments, or chapter markers.
criteria.reasoning: Observed integer-millisecond guidance and Snap options for 10 ms grid, token boundaries, and chapter markers. Evidence: cf_2-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_6
criteria.description: Dialogue overlaps require declared cross-talk lanes, and fades/crossfades obey clip bounds.
criteria.reasoning: Mix validation visibly reports no undeclared dialogue overlaps and confirms fades fit clip bounds and crossfades overlap neighbors. Evidence: cf_13-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_7
criteria.description: Keyboard and mobile numeric operations equal pointer gestures for inserting, moving, trimming, splitting, and rippling clips.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Keyboard/mobile parity for all insert, move, trim, split, and ripple operations was not directly observed. Evidence: cf_2-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_8
criteria.description: Active dialogue instances derive transcript tokens inside source ranges.
criteria.reasoning: Selected Clip 1 visibly derived 16 transcript tokens from its 0–10000 ms source range. Evidence: cf_8-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_9
criteria.description: Editors can mark tokens as included/excluded, correct fixture errors with provenance, and bind exact spans to show-note claims/quotes.
criteria.reasoning: Transcript UI exposed token inclusion toggles and span binding; selecting “Welcome” and binding it produced a visible bound citation and history entry. Evidence: cf_9-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_10
criteria.description: Ripple edits shift episode times without changing source times, and orphaned citations block approval.
criteria.reasoning: Ripple move produced history stating episode times shifted while source times remained unchanged; citation UI states unbound citations block approval. Evidence: cf_10-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_11
criteria.description: A narrative outline supports cold open, introduction, three chapters, transition, and credits blocks with title, role, time range, required speakers/topics, and show-note summary.
criteria.reasoning: Narrative Outline visibly contained Cold Open, Introduction, three chapters, Transition, and Credits with ranges, speakers/topics, and summaries. Evidence: cf_11-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_12
criteria.description: Reordering blocks moves their clip groups under explicit ripple preview. Chapters cannot overlap and must cover the approved timeline.
criteria.reasoning: Reordering opened and confirmed an explicit Ripple Preview; UI stated blocks remain contiguous with no overlaps and full coverage. Evidence: cf_12-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_13
criteria.description: Clip gain and lane automation points produce deterministic sampled loudness/peak fixture values via linear interpolation, and curves drag on time/value axes.
criteria.reasoning: Mix Automation visibly provided draggable time/value axes, automation points, linear-interpolation sampled loudness/peak tables, and lane controls. Evidence: cf_13-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_14
criteria.description: A validator checks dialogue target band, jump thresholds, clipping, music-under-dialogue ducking, and fade continuity.
criteria.reasoning: Validation Findings visibly covered dialogue target band, jump thresholds, clipping, music ducking, fade continuity, and cross-talk. Evidence: cf_13-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_15
criteria.description: Every included source requires allowed usage, territory fixture, attribution text, and expiry after publish date.
criteria.reasoning: Rights Review visibly checked allowed usage, territory, attribution, expiry after the publish date, and showed named blocker/state information. Evidence: cf_15-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_16
criteria.description: Transcript/citation, editorial, rights, accessibility, and master approvals freeze exact cut checksums; any material edit marks affected approvals stale.
criteria.reasoning: Approvals visibly froze a checksum; after a real 10 ms trim, transcript/editorial/accessibility changed to stale with the affected checksum transition. Evidence: cf_16-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_17
criteria.description: Users can fork cuts to compare clip membership/order/ranges, transcript, chapters, mix, duration, rights, and notes, then merge property/time-range conflicts.
criteria.reasoning: Forked the cut, compared clips/transcript/chapters/mix/duration/rights/notes, then created a clip time conflict exposing Keep Main, Keep Fork, and Apply Merge Into Main controls. Evidence: cf_17-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_18
criteria.description: The first batch deterministically fails transcript timestamp and RSS enclosure checks; a retry failed-only preserves successful outputs and attempts.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Clicking Render was blocked by missing approvals before any batch ran, so deterministic transcript/RSS failures and failed-only retry could not be completed. Evidence: cf_18-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_19
criteria.description: Export produces Canonical JSON schemaVersion podcast-episode-package/v1, CSV EDL/transcript ledger, WebVTT transcript, RSS XML item, Markdown show notes/credits, and SVG timeline/loudness report.
criteria.reasoning: Export All visibly produced Canonical JSON with schemaVersion podcast-episode-package/v1, EDL CSV, transcript ledger CSV, WebVTT, RSS XML, Markdown, and Timeline/Loudness SVG. Evidence: cf_19-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_20
criteria.description: Import reconstructs state exactly and rejects invalid artifacts. Canonical re-export changes only exportedAt while CSV/VTT/RSS/Markdown/SVG remain byte-identical.
criteria.reasoning: Imported the last export successfully, rejected invalid schemaVersion with named validation errors, and re-export history reported companions byte-identical with only exportedAt changed. Evidence: cf_20-shared.png

criteria.name: spacing_and_sizing_follow_scale
criteria.description: Spacing and sizing follow the design system's scale, with no arbitrary one-off values visible in computed styles.
criteria.reasoning: Consistent compact spacing and repeated control sizing are visible across the three-pane workspace. Evidence: initial-view-shared.png

criteria.name: typography_matches_spec
criteria.description: Typography matches the spec for all headings and body copy.
criteria.reasoning: Headings, labels, and body copy use a consistent sans-serif system with visibly distinct sizes and weights. Evidence: initial-view-shared.png

criteria.name: layout_matches_reference
criteria.description: Layout matches reference screenshots to within a small tolerance at all specified breakpoints; fidelity tasks achieve near pixel-parity.
criteria.reasoning: Desktop view shows a coherent header, source bin, central workspace, and approval sidebar composition. Evidence: initial-view-shared.png

criteria.name: specified_state_changes_animate
criteria.description: Transitions or animations are applied to the state changes the spec calls out, such as add, delete, filter, or view switch.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Switching from Timeline to Transcript changed views immediately; no meaningful content transition was observed. Evidence: transcript-view-shared.png

criteria.name: responsive_behavior_matches_reference
criteria.description: Responsive behavior matches reference patterns.
criteria.reasoning: At compact width, the header wraps and workspace navigation remains usable with horizontal scrolling. Evidence: compact-shared.png

criteria.name: control_styling_matches_spec
criteria.description: Button and form radii, padding, and shadows conform to the spec.
criteria.reasoning: Buttons and inputs are consistently styled with custom fills, borders, compact padding, and 4px radii. Evidence: theme-state-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_8
criteria.description: Active dialogue instances derive transcript tokens inside source ranges.
criteria.reasoning: Selected Clip 1 visibly derives 16 transcript tokens from its 0–10000 ms source range. Evidence: cf_2-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_9
criteria.description: Editors can mark tokens as included/excluded, correct fixture errors with provenance, and bind exact spans to show-note claims/quotes.
criteria.reasoning: Transcript view visibly supports token inclusion toggles, span binding, and citation binding; the live history records a bound “Welcome” span. Evidence: cf_4-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_10
criteria.description: Ripple edits shift episode times without changing source times, and orphaned citations block approval.
criteria.reasoning: A live move shifted episode time from 28000–38000 to 29000–39000 ms while source time stayed 0–10000 ms; the citation panel visibly reports orphaned citations as approval blockers. Evidence: cf_16-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_11
criteria.description: A narrative outline supports cold open, introduction, three chapters, transition, and credits blocks with title, role, time range, required speakers/topics, and show-note summary.
criteria.reasoning: Narrative Outline visibly contains cold open, introduction, three chapters, transition, and credits with ranges, speakers/topics, and summaries. Evidence: chapters.png

criteria.name: cf_12
criteria.description: Reordering blocks moves their clip groups under explicit ripple preview. Chapters cannot overlap and must cover the approved timeline.
criteria.reasoning: A real chapter reorder opened an explicit Ripple Preview showing contiguous before/after ranges and confirmed no overlaps/full coverage. Evidence: cf_12-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_13
criteria.description: Clip gain and lane automation points produce deterministic sampled loudness/peak fixture values via linear interpolation, and curves drag on time/value axes.
criteria.reasoning: Mix view visibly provides lane automation points, time/dB axes, linear-interpolation sampled loudness/peak values, and deterministic validation data. Evidence: mix.png

criteria.name: cf_14
criteria.description: A validator checks dialogue target band, jump thresholds, clipping, music-under-dialogue ducking, and fade continuity.
criteria.reasoning: Mix validation visibly reports dialogue target band, jump thresholds, clipping, ducking, fade continuity, and cross-talk checks. Evidence: mix.png

criteria.name: cf_15
criteria.description: Every included source requires allowed usage, territory fixture, attribution text, and expiry after publish date.
criteria.reasoning: Rights Review visibly checks usage, territory, attribution, expiry, and names failing rights states/attribution gaps. Evidence: rights.png

criteria.name: cf_16
criteria.description: Transcript/citation, editorial, rights, accessibility, and master approvals freeze exact cut checksums; any material edit marks affected approvals stale.
criteria.reasoning: Approvals visibly freeze checksum 48406c0c; after a live move, transcript/editorial/accessibility approvals became stale with an explicit causal checksum explanation. Evidence: cf_16-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_17
criteria.description: Users can fork cuts to compare clip membership/order/ranges, transcript, chapters, mix, duration, rights, and notes, then merge property/time-range conflicts.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: forking and comparison were observed, but no divergent property/time-range conflict state and merge resolution could be reached and verified. Evidence: cf_17-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_18
criteria.description: The first batch deterministically fails transcript timestamp and RSS enclosure checks; a retry failed-only preserves successful outputs and attempts.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: render attempts were blocked by unapproved rights/master requirements, so the failed-only retry workflow and preservation of successful outputs could not be completed. Evidence: cf_18-shared.png

criteria.name: cf_19
criteria.description: Export produces Canonical JSON schemaVersion podcast-episode-package/v1, CSV EDL/transcript ledger, WebVTT transcript, RSS XML item, Markdown show notes/credits, and SVG timeline/loudness report.
criteria.reasoning: Export All visibly produced canonical JSON with schemaVersion podcast-episode-package/v1, EDL CSV, transcript ledger CSV, WebVTT, RSS XML, Markdown show notes, and SVG timeline/loudness output. Evidence: cf_19-shared.png

criteria.name: mo_1
criteria.description: Clip travel/trim/ripple, transcript/chapter shifts, and automation/loudness changes visually animate.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: clip movement and crossfade reached their final states, but no transient visual animation for travel/trim/ripple, chapter shifts, or automation/loudness changes was directly observed. Evidence: cf_4-shared.png

criteria.name: mo_2
criteria.description: Stale approval transitions and render retries explain cause with causal motion.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: stale approvals explained their checksum cause, but a render retry transition could not be reached because approvals blocked rendering. Evidence: cf_16-shared.png

criteria.name: mo_3
criteria.description: Reduced motion retains before/after time/value/status deltas instantly without animation.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: reduced-motion was confirmed, but a complete before/after operation with verified instantaneous value/status delta was not successfully exercised. Evidence: mo_3-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_use_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and section titles, they use consistent capitalization (one convention — title case or sentence case — throughout).
criteria.reasoning: Headings consistently use title case, including “Source Bin,” “Branch Cuts,” and “History Log.” Evidence: copy-main-shared.png

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they are specific verbs ('Add expense', 'Start session'), not generic ('Submit', 'OK') when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: Actions use contextual labels such as “Insert,” “Fork This Cut,” “Edit Tokens,” and “Remove Clip,” without vague labels like “Submit” or “OK.” Evidence: copy-main-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders error messages, they name the problem and the fix ('Amount must be a positive number'), not just a rejection ('Invalid').
criteria.reasoning: The forbidden-rights error identifies the problem and explains the fix: “clear the rights record before inserting it.” Evidence: copy-error-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states, the copy explains what belongs there and how to add it.
criteria.reasoning: Empty states explain what is missing and what to do next, such as “fork the cut to start comparing” and “every edit lands here with its revision number.” Evidence: copy-main-shared.png

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or marketing copy, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered copy is clear, grammatical, and professional throughout the observed UI. Evidence: copy-main-shared.png

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places, terminology is consistent (not 'task' here and 'todo' there).
criteria.reasoning: The UI consistently refers to clips, branches, citations, rights, and history using the same terms across panels. Evidence: copy-main-shared.png

criteria.name: numbers_dates_and_units_are_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders numbers, dates, and units, formatting is consistent (same date format, same decimal precision, units labeled).
criteria.reasoning: Durations use consistent timestamp formatting, while editable timing values consistently identify milliseconds and rights expiry uses labeled days. Evidence: copy-main-shared.png

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation and success messages, they state what happened ('Entry saved'), not vague affirmations.
criteria.reasoning: The confirmation explicitly states what happened: “Clip 1 inserted into dialogue at 28000 ms.” Evidence: copy-success-shared.png

criteria.name: placeholder_copy_is_visible
criteria.description: Lorem ipsum or placeholder text ('TODO', 'Lorem', 'asdf', or template variable names such as '{{title}}') is visible anywhere in the shipped UI.
criteria.reasoning: No Lorem ipsum, TODO, template variables, or similar placeholder copy was observed. Evidence: copy-main-shared.png

criteria.name: prominent_copy_has_errors
criteria.description: Spelling or grammatical errors appear in prominent copy such as headings, hero text, or primary buttons.
criteria.reasoning: No spelling or grammatical errors were observed in prominent headings, hero copy, or primary buttons. Evidence: copy-main-shared.png

criteria.name: concept_names_are_inconsistent
criteria.description: The same concept is named inconsistently across surfaces.
criteria.reasoning: No inconsistent naming of the same concept was observed across the rendered surfaces. Evidence: copy-main-shared.png

criteria.name: copy_is_truncated_or_overflowing
criteria.description: Truncated or overflowing text renders copy unreadable.
criteria.reasoning: Observed copy remains readable; transcript teaser ellipses are intentional previews rather than unreadable overflow. Evidence: copy-main-shared.png

criteria.name: writing_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in rendered copy quality that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No significant browser-observable copy-quality defect outside the covered criteria was observed. Evidence: copy-main-shared.png
